review_notes: Residual-blind source review only. I used the supplied README/USAGE, source evidence, preflight observables, component manifest, path/interloper audit, normalization theorem, epsilon-cap protocol theorem, obligations, and forbidden-input table. I did not use rotation residuals, endpoint RMSE, baseline ranks, wrong-family Tau scores, post-hoc cap changes, or foreground/path rescue. Verdict: the high-spin and edge-on PV/envelope evidence is strong enough to carry the Xi_t K_t(R) source route forward as a caveated interval/control manifest candidate, but not as an endpoint permission. High-spin settling is accepted from lambda_spin=0.15 plus low-density extended H I. Edge-on PV is accepted with caveat: it is admissible as a time-slice/readout proxy because envelope/PV treatment is source-required, but it remains projection-linked. The envelope ramp from R_d/R_opt toward R_HI is accepted with caveat because R_HI source and SPARC values are consistent and source-frozen, while the exact ramp remains manifest-level protocol. Approaching/receding asymmetry should remain only as a caveated phase/interval component, not a standalone route driver. The path term should remain zero because foreground/path evidence is not established. epsilon_cap=0.035 may be carried only as a predeclared protocol cap within the small-mismatch bound, not as a universal Tau Core constant.
